vars.name: MedicalHistory
vars.doc:
MedicalHistory that is created once for each patient.
Stores things like diabetic, hypertensive, sickler, CKD patient etc.
We use booleans to track each chronic illness to allow us analyze
the cardiovascular disease risk of this patient and detect potential
drug interactions.

vars.name: data
vars.doc:
ChartPrescription is used for an inpaitents and outpatients on progressive treatment.
The distinction between the two is that inpatients are admitted and outpatients are not.
The prescription stores the drug, route, dose, frequency, duration and total doses etc.
We differentiate between inpatient and outpatient prescriptions because inpatients are
admitted. We look inside admissions table for a patient with a visit_id
to determine if the patient is an inpatient or outpatient.

vars.name: paymentTypes
vars.doc:
PaymentType represents the different types of prices for an item.
e.g. Cash, UAP, SanCare, Liberty, Staff Insurance, etc.
This decouples the price from the item, allowing for multiple prices for an item
and also for the creation of new price types without changing the item.

vars.name: items
vars.doc:
Inventory is the Combined inventory item stored in the database.
Inventory items that are drugs store their quantity, expiry data and batch numbers
in seperate batches.
Consumables store their quantity directly, just like their expiry date.

For all other billable types, quantity, batch_number and expiry_date is not important.

	id | name | batch_number | quantity | cost_price | selling_price |  payment_method   | expiry_date | type

vars.name: paymentTypes
vars.doc:
PaymentType represents the different types of prices for an item.
e.g. Cash, UAP, SanCare, Liberty, Staff Insurance, etc.
This decouples the price from the item, allowing for multiple prices for an item
and also for the creation of new price types without changing the item.

vars.name: batch
vars.doc:
DrugBatch represents a specific batch of a drug
A drug can have multiple batches with different expiry dates and quantities.
Uniqueness is enforced on the combination of InventoryItemID and BatchNumber & ExpiryDate.

vars.name: paymentTypes
vars.doc:
PaymentType represents the different types of prices for an item.
e.g. Cash, UAP, SanCare, Liberty, Staff Insurance, etc.
This decouples the price from the item, allowing for multiple prices for an item
and also for the creation of new price types without changing the item.

IsCashier returns true if the user is a cashier.
It will be true if the user is an Admin or superuser.

funcMaps.name: AllowEditingQuantity
funcMaps.doc:
AllowEditingQuantity returns true if the user is allowed to edit the quantity of a billable item.
Editing is allowed if the user is not a cashier, the bill has not been paid in full, and the bill is less than 12 hours old.

funcMaps.name: paymentIncomplete
funcMaps.doc: PaymentIncomplete returns true if the patient has an outstanding balance on their bill.

funcMaps.name: AppointmentExpired

funcMaps.name: TotalPayment

isPrescriber function that checks if the user is
the prescriber of the billable.

funcMaps.doc: HasPermission returns true if the user has the specified permission string.

funcMaps.name: ParseUnits

funcMaps.doc:
ExtractVisitID splits s on VisitNameSplitter token and returns first part which is the visit ID.
Format: "visitId - patientName"

funcMaps.name: ExtractVisitName
funcMaps.doc:
ExtractVisitName splits s on VisitNameSplitter and returns the second part which is the patient name.
Format: "visitId - patientName"

funcMaps.doc: ShouldQueueOTC returns true if the user is an admin or if the visit is within 12 hours

admin.Visits.name: Outpatient
admin.Visits.doc: Departments in which the visit was created.

cbc.FivePartCBC.name: NormalRange
cbc.FivePartCBC.doc: Store each report with its normal range

cbc.ThreePartCBC.name: NormalRange
cbc.ThreePartCBC.doc: Store each report with its normal range

labreports.GeneralRange.name: Value
labreports.GeneralRange.doc:
Value implements the driver.Valuer interface
This method is called when storing the GeneralRange in the database

labreports.GeneralRange.name: Scan
labreports.GeneralRange.doc:
Scan implements the sql.Scanner interface
This method is called when reading the GeneralRange from the database

labreports.GeneralRange.name: GormDataType
labreports.GeneralRange.doc: Returns the data type to be used by GORM.

labreports.LFTs.name: TotalProtein
labreports.LFTs.doc: Total protein is the sum of albumin and globulins.

labreports.LFTs.name: Albumin
labreports.LFTs.doc:
Albumin is a protein made by the liver. It helps keep the blood from leaking out of blood vessels.
Hypoalbuminemia can indicate liver disease or kidney disease or malnutrition.

labreports.LFTs.name: Globulins
labreports.LFTs.doc:
Globulins are a group of proteins in the blood, including antibodies, enzymes, and other proteins.
High globulin levels can indicate liver disease, kidney disease, or chronic inflammatory disease.
It can also be elevated in multiple myeloma in which case it's called paraproteinemia(Bence Jones protein).

labreports.LFTs.name: AGRatio
labreports.LFTs.doc:
A/G ratio is the ratio of albumin to globulins. It's used to help diagnose liver disease.
Normal values are between 1.0 and 2.0.
Low A/G ratio can indicate liver disease, kidney disease(nephrotic syndrome), or malnutrition.
High A/G ratio can indicate dehydration, severe burns, or a rare genetic disorder called alpha-1
antitrypsin deficiency, eating disorders, or autoimmune diseases.

labreports.LFTs.name: TotalBilirubin
labreports.LFTs.doc:
Bilirubin is a yellow compound that occurs in the normal catabolic pathway that breaks down heme in vertebrates.
A bilirubinemia presents with jaundice, a yellowing of the skin and eyes.

labreports.LFTs.name: DirectBilirubin
labreports.LFTs.doc:
Same as conjugated bilirubin. It's the bilirubin that is directly conjugated to glucuronic acid.
in the liver and excreted in the bile. High levels of direct bilirubin can indicate liver disease.

labreports.LFTs.name: IndirectBilirubin
labreports.LFTs.doc:
Same as unconjugated bilirubin. It's the bilirubin that is not directly conjugated to glucuronic acid.
Unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia is usually due to hemolysis or Gilbert's syndrome.

labreports.LFTs.name: ALT
labreports.LFTs.doc:
ALT: Alanine amino transferase. It's an enzyme that is found mostly in the liver.
Elevated ALT indicated direct hepatocellular damage. e.g in hepatitis.

labreports.LFTs.name: ALP
labreports.LFTs.doc:
ALP: Alkaline phosphatase. It's an enzyme that is found in many tissues throughout the body.
Elevated ALP can indicate liver disease or bone disorders.

labreports.LFTs.name: AST
labreports.LFTs.doc:
AST: Aspartate amino transferase. It's an enzyme that is found in the liver, heart, muscles, and other tissues.
Elevated AST can indicate liver damage, heart attack, or muscle damage.

labreports.LFTs.name: GGT
labreports.LFTs.doc:
GGT: Gamma-glutamyl transferase. It's an enzyme that is found in the liver, kidney, pancreas, and other tissues.
Elevated GGT can indicate liver disease, alcohol abuse, or bile duct obstruction.

labreports.LipidProfile.name: Triglycerides
labreports.LipidProfile.doc:
Triglycerides are a type of fat found in your blood. Your body uses them for energy.
Hypertriglyceridemia can be caused by obesity, uncontrolled diabetes, or hypothyroidism.

labreports.LipidProfile.name: TotalCholesterol
labreports.LipidProfile.doc: Total cholesterol is the sum of all types of cholesterol in your blood.

labreports.LipidProfile.name: HdlCholesterol
labreports.LipidProfile.doc:
HDL: High-density lipoprotein. It's known as the "good" cholesterol.
Low HDL levels can increase the risk of heart disease.

labreports.LipidProfile.name: LdlCholesterol
labreports.LipidProfile.doc:
LDL: Low-density lipoprotein. It's known as the "bad" cholesterol.
High LDL levels can increase the risk of heart disease.

labreports.LipidProfile.name: GSP
labreports.LipidProfile.doc:
GSP: Glycated serum protein. It's a test that measures the amount of sugar that is attached to proteins in the blood.
High GSP levels can indicate diabetes or other conditions that affect blood sugar levels.

labreports.LipidProfile.name: Glucose
labreports.LipidProfile.doc: Glucose is a type of sugar that is found in the blood. It's the body's main source of energy.

labreports.LipidProfile.name: NonHdlCholesterol
labreports.LipidProfile.doc:
Represents LDL, VLDL, and IDL cholesterol.
Non-HDL cholesterol is a better predictor of heart disease risk than LDL cholesterol.
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10652132/

labreports.LipidProfile.name: CholesterolHDLRatio
labreports.LipidProfile.doc:
Serum cholesterol/HDL ratio is a ratio of total cholesterol to HDL cholesterol.
It's used to help predict the risk of heart disease.
A high ratio can indicate an increased risk of heart disease.

labreports.RFTs.name: SerumCreatinine
labreports.RFTs.doc:
Creatinine is a waste product produced by muscles from the breakdown of a compound called creatine.
Normal creatinine levels are between 0.84 to 1.21 milligrams per deciliter (mg/dL)
High creatinine levels can indicate kidney disease or other conditions that affect kidney function.
Creatinine levels can also increase temporarily as a result of muscle injury.

labreports.RFTs.name: BUN
labreports.RFTs.doc:
BUN: Blood Urea Nitrogen. It's a waste product that forms when your body breaks down protein.
Normal BUN levels are between 7 to 20 milligrams per deciliter (mg/dL)
High BUN levels can indicate kidney disease, heart failure, dehydration, or a urinary tract obstruction.
Uremia is a condition in which there is a high level of urea in the blood.

models.Billable.name: BatchID
models.Billable.doc: BatchID is the batch of the drug if it is a drug.

models.DeliveryNote.name: Sex
models.DeliveryNote.doc: Sex of baby. Male or Female

models.DeliveryNote.name: ImmediatePostpartumCare
models.DeliveryNote.doc: Any other postpartum care given to both monther and baby

models.Deposit.name: Bank
models.Deposit.doc: Bank is the bank the money was deposited to

models.Deposit.name: AccountNumber
models.Deposit.doc: Account Number is the account the money was deposited to

models.Deposit.name: Issuer
models.Deposit.doc: Issuer is the person(cashier) who issued the money to the depositor

models.Deposit.name: Depositor
models.Deposit.doc: Depositor is the person who banked the money

models.Deposit.name: CreatedAt
models.Deposit.doc: CreateAt is the time the deposit was made

models.Expense.name: AmountSpent
models.Expense.doc:
AmountSpent is the total amount spent.
AmountSpent is the sum of Charges and NetAmount.

models.Expense.name: Charges
models.Expense.doc: Charges is the amount lost in charges during the payment

models.Expense.name: NetAmount
models.Expense.doc:
NetAmount is the amount after deducting charges
This is a computed field.

models.Expense.name: Cashier
models.Expense.doc:
Cashier is the person who spent the money.
This is registered when creating the expense.

models.Expense.name: Reason
models.Expense.doc: Reason is the reason for the expense, e.g. Rent, Salaries, etc.

models.Expense.name: Authorizer
models.Expense.doc: Authorizer is the person who authorized the expense.

models.Expense.name: Source
models.Expense.doc: Source is the source of the money spent, e.g. Owner, Bank, etc.

models.Expense.name: PaymentMethod
models.Expense.doc: PaymentMethod is the medium through which the money was spent, e.g. Bank, Cash, etc.

models.Expense.name: Beneficiary
models.Expense.doc: Beneficiary is the person who received the money

models.Expense.name: CreatedAt
models.Expense.doc: CreatedAt is the time the expense was made

models.ExternalRevenue.name: GrossAmount
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: GrossAmount is the total amount received

models.ExternalRevenue.name: NetAmount
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: NetAmount is the amount after deducting charges and taxes

models.ExternalRevenue.name: ChargesAndTaxes
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: ChargesAndTaxes is the amount deducted as charges and taxes

models.ExternalRevenue.name: Channel
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: channel is the medium through which the money was received, e.g. Bank, Cash, etc.

models.ExternalRevenue.name: Service
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: Service is the reason for the revenue, e.g. Loan, Investment, Sale of Asset, etc.

models.ExternalRevenue.name: Source
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: Source is the person or org who issued the money

models.ExternalRevenue.name: Cashier
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: Cashier is the person who received the money

models.ExternalRevenue.name: CreatedAt
models.ExternalRevenue.doc: CreatedAt is the date the revenue was received

models.GeneralInventory.name: Description
models.GeneralInventory.doc: Description is a brief description of the item

models.GeneralInventory.name: Category
models.GeneralInventory.doc: Category is the category of the item, e.g. Stationery, Cleaning supplies, Lab reagents, etc.

models.GeneralInventory.name: Quantity
models.GeneralInventory.doc: Quantity is the number of items in stock in the unit of measurement.

models.GeneralInventory.name: Units
models.GeneralInventory.doc: Units is the unit of measurement for the item, e.g. reams, boxes, litres, etc.

models.GeneralInventory.name: CostPrice
models.GeneralInventory.doc: CostPrice is the cost price of the item

models.GeneralInventory.name: RecordedBy
models.GeneralInventory.doc: RecordedBy is the user who added the item to the inventory

models.GeneralInventory.name: ExpiryDate
models.GeneralInventory.doc: ExpiryDate is the date the item expires. NULL for items that do not expire.

models.GeneralInventory.name: Remarks
models.GeneralInventory.doc: Remarks is any additional information about the item

models.GeneralInventory.name: CreatedAt
models.GeneralInventory.doc: CreatedAt is the time the item was added to the inventory

models.GeneralInventory.name: UpdatedAt
models.GeneralInventory.doc: UpdatedAt is the time the item was last updated

models.GeneralInventory.name: IsExpired
models.GeneralInventory.doc:
IsExpired returns true if the item has expired.
If the expiry date is not set, the item is assumed not expired.

models.GeneralInventory.name: ValidCategories
models.GeneralInventory.doc:
ValidCategories returns a list of valid categories for GeneralInventory items.
This is used to validate the category of an item.
This is more flexible than creating a a postgres enum type.

models.GeneralInventory.name: HasValidCategory
models.GeneralInventory.doc: HasValidCategory returns true if the item has a valid category.

models.Hospital.name: Number

models.InsuranceCard.name: PatientID
models.InsuranceCard.doc: PatientID is the ID of the patient who holds the insurance card.

models.InsuranceCard.name: PaymentTypeID
models.InsuranceCard.doc: PaymentTypeID is the ID of the payment type used to pay for the insurance card.

models.InsuranceCard.name: PaymentType
models.InsuranceCard.doc: PaymentType is the payment type used to pay for the insurance card.

models.InsuranceCard.name: Patient
models.InsuranceCard.doc: Patient is the patient who holds the insurance card.

models.InsuranceCard.name: Active
models.InsuranceCard.doc: Active is a boolean that determines if the insurance card is active.

models.InternalIssuedItem.name: ItemID
models.InternalIssuedItem.doc: ItemID is the foreign key to the inventory_items table.

models.InternalIssuedItem.name: Item
models.InternalIssuedItem.doc: Item is the Inventory item this issue represents.

models.InternalIssuedItem.name: BatchID
models.InternalIssuedItem.doc: BatchID is the FK for the drug batch. Null is not drug.

models.InternalIssuedItem.name: BatchNumber
models.InternalIssuedItem.doc: The Batch number of the drug.

models.InternalIssuedItem.name: Department
models.InternalIssuedItem.doc:
The department that is taking the items from pharmacy e.g Consultation Rooms.
Valid ones are defined by the system.

models.InventoryItem.name: Name
models.InventoryItem.doc: The inventory item name. It must be unique.

models.InventoryItem.name: Type
models.InventoryItem.doc: Type is the BillableType of the item.

models.InventoryItem.name: CostPrice
models.InventoryItem.doc:
CostPrice is the unit cost of each item or otherwise best called "rate" in commerce.
TODO: The current implementation has a limitation because drugs of different batches have the same cost price.
TODO: The aim if to add a cost price field per batch to fix this issue.

models.InventoryItem.name: Dept
models.InventoryItem.doc:
Dept is the inventory department. It is used to differentiate between lab investigations, radiology tests
pharmacy items (drugs and consumables), nursing care etc.
So when we make a bill, we know where to send it.

models.InventoryItem.name: Quantity
models.InventoryItem.doc:
The Quantity of items in stock. Like CostPrice, this ONLY makes sense for Consumables.
Drug quantity is stored under each batch. BillableTypes other than Consumable ignore this field all together.

models.InventoryItem.name: CreatedAt
models.InventoryItem.doc: The time when the item was inserted into the database.

models.InventoryItem.name: ExpiryDate
models.InventoryItem.doc:
Item expiry date. Expiry dates for drugs are stored per batch, so this is only useful for consumables ONLY.
It will otherwise be the zero value 0001-01-01.

models.InventoryItem.name: MinAlertQuantity
models.InventoryItem.doc: Quantity that should be in stock before an alert is raised.

models.InventoryItem.name: Batches
models.InventoryItem.doc:
DrugBatches for the item (if it is a drug)
For backwards compatibility, we maintain the Quantity Field and the ExpiryDate field here.
Like wise other items that are not drugs don't have DrugBatches.

models.InventoryItem.name: HasValidExpiryDate
models.InventoryItem.doc: HasValidExpiryDate returns true if the item has a valid expiry date.

models.InventoryItem.name: IsExpired
models.InventoryItem.doc:
IsExpired returns true if the item expired.
If the item is not a drug or consumable, it always returns false.

models.InventoryItem.name: Price
models.InventoryItem.doc:
Price returns the inventory item selling price for the given payment type.
If the payment type does not exist, it will return 0 and an error.

models.InventoryItem.name: PriceByID
models.InventoryItem.doc:
PriceByID returns the selling price of the inventory item for the given payment ID.
If the payment type id does not exist, it will return 0 and an error.

models.InventoryItem.name: IsDrug
models.InventoryItem.doc: IsDrug returns true if the item.Type == enums.BillableTypeDrug.

models.InventoryItem.name: IsConsumable
models.InventoryItem.doc: IsConsumable returns true if the item.Type == enums.BillableTypeConsumable.

models.InventoryItem.name: IsDrugOrConsumable
models.InventoryItem.doc: IsDrugOrConsumable returns true if item.IsDrug() || item.IsConsumable().

models.Invoice.name: InvoiceTotal
models.Invoice.doc: Total amount spent on the invoice.

models.Invoice.name: AmountPaid
models.Invoice.doc: Total amount paid to the supplier on the invoice.

models.Invoice.name: Balance
models.Invoice.doc:
Debt owed to the supplier.
This is computed as InvoiceTotal - AmountPaid.

models.Invoice.name: Cashier
models.Invoice.doc: The Cashier who made the purchase.

models.LabReport.name: TestID
models.LabReport.doc: TestID is the foreignKey for the inventory_items.id

models.LabReport.name: Test
models.LabReport.doc: Test maps the fk TestID to the inventory item.

models.LabReport.name: RequestID
models.LabReport.doc: request_id is the Foreign Key for billables.id

models.LabReport.name: Request
models.LabReport.doc: Request is the matching  --billable-- for this report.

models.LabReport.name: TestName
models.LabReport.doc: Full name of the investigation name.

models.LabReport.name: Type
models.LabReport.doc: Stores the department where this report belongs.

models.LabReport.name: Report
models.LabReport.doc:
Report stores the JSON data of the report body.
It can the General report(also used by radiology), CBC3Part, CBC5Part

models.LabReport.name: Comment
models.LabReport.doc: Comment is a string field to store any additional information to the report

models.LabReport.name: MachineGenerated
models.LabReport.doc:
MachineGenerated is a boolean field to indicate if the report was generated by a machine
or manually entered by a technician. Useful for filtering reports in the frontend
and for determining if to update normal ranges.

models.LabReport.name: NormalRanges
models.LabReport.doc:
NormalRanges stores the report normal ranges.
This does not represent CBC ranges because CBCs store their own normal ranges.
GORM will save it appropriately because this field implements
the sql.valuer and driver.Valuer interfaces.

models.LabReport.name: NormalRangesOrDefault
models.LabReport.doc: NormalRangesOrDefault returns the report normal ranges or default normal ranges if they are nil.

models.LabReport.name: AsGeneral
models.LabReport.doc: AsGeneral adds specific casting functions for each report type

models.LabReport.name: AsRFTs
models.LabReport.doc: AsRFTs converts the report.Report to RFTs.

models.MaternityVisit.name: TTVaccine
models.MaternityVisit.doc: Whether has received Td vaccine in this pregnancy

models.Patient.name: ReferredBy
models.Patient.doc: Person who referred the patient to the facility.

models.Patient.name: InsuranceCards
models.Patient.doc: Add support for multiple insurance cards.

models.Patient.name: AfterCreate
models.Patient.doc: AfterCreate gorm hook that sends the new patient notification.

models.Patient.name: CurrentAge
models.Patient.doc: CurrentAge returns age of the patient from the current date and time.

models.Patient.name: AgeSince
models.Patient.doc: AgeSince returns the age of the patient from visitTime.

models.ReportTemplate.name: CreatorID
models.ReportTemplate.doc: UserID of the user who created the template.

models.StockIn.name: ItemID
models.StockIn.doc: ItemID is the foreign key representing the ID of the item in inventory_items table.

models.StockIn.name: BatchID
models.StockIn.doc:
BatchID is the foreign key representing the ID of the drug batch.
It is NULL or 0 if the item is not a drug.

models.StockIn.name: InvoiceID
models.StockIn.doc: InvoiceID is the Foreign Key in the invoices table. This links the stock ins to the same invoice.

models.StockIn.name: QuantityIn
models.StockIn.doc:
This is the absolute Quantity of stocked items.
This is the amount deducted from the stock (i.e batch for drugs) when the item is stocked
or re-stocked. This is not the BULK unit.

models.StockIn.name: QuantityInBulk
models.StockIn.doc: Quantity stocked in bulk. This is optional.

models.StockIn.name: BulkUnit
models.StockIn.doc: BulkUnit stores the units for QuantityInBulk. E.g Boxes, Pcks, etc. This is optional.

models.StockIn.name: ExpiryDate
models.StockIn.doc:
ExpiryDate stores the item expiry date.
It will update the inventory_items.expiry_date field for matching item or drug batch.

models.StockIn.name: CreatedAt
models.StockIn.doc:
CreatedAt is the time the item was created.
It should not be NULL. It is kept as NULL for backward compatibility.

models.StockIn.name: Invoice
models.StockIn.doc: Invoice this stocked item belong to. Defines the FK relation.

models.StockIn.name: Item
models.StockIn.doc: Item is the Inventory item this stockin represents.

models.TbScreening.name: Cough
models.TbScreening.doc: cough > 2 weeks or for HIV patients (any duration)

models.TbScreening.name: PoorWeightGain
models.TbScreening.doc: Poor weight gain in child in last 1 month.

models.TbScreening.name: Classification
models.TbScreening.doc:
Classification
Definite case of TB, Probable TB, Definitely NOT TB.

models.TbScreening.name: Contact
models.TbScreening.doc: Contact with an adult with PTB(for children < 5 years)

models.TbScreening.name: SputumCollectedDate
models.TbScreening.doc: Date sputum was collected if at all.

models.TbScreening.name: SputumReceivedDate
models.TbScreening.doc: Date sputum was received if at all.

models.TbScreening.name: Visit
models.TbScreening.doc: To make sure a FK constraint is enforced.

models.TbScreening.name: Recommendation
models.TbScreening.doc: Recommendation returns the recommendation based on the algorithm

models.User.name: LastLogout
models.User.doc: Last logout time of the day

models.User.name: HashPassword
models.User.doc:
HashPassword hashes the user.Password using bcrypt with a cost of 14.
It updates the user password in-place.
Will fail is hashing fails.

models.User.name: BeforeCreate
models.User.doc: BeforeCreate gorm hook that calls user.HashPassword.

models.User.name: AfterCreate
models.User.doc: AfterCreate gorm hook that sends the new user notification.

models.User.name: HasPermission
models.User.doc:
HasPermission checks if user has permissions to perform certain tasks or access resources
If exact, superuser checks and Admin permissions are skipped.
Otherwise they will always return true.

models.User.name: AssignPermissions
models.User.doc:
AssignPermissions assigns new permissions to user.
This will clear old permissions. If you want to create multiple permissions, pass them at once.

models.User.name: AssignPermissionStrings
models.User.doc: AssignPermissionStrings assigns new permissions to user as strings.

models.User.name: AppendPermission
models.User.doc:
AppendPermission appends new permissions to user as strings.
Returns an error if permission is not valid

models.User.name: RevokePermission
models.User.doc: RevokePermission revokes the specified permission from the user.

models.User.name: ClearAllPermissions
models.User.doc: ClearAllPermissions clears all permissions from the user.

models.User.name: Permissions
models.User.doc: Permissions returns user permissions as a slice of strings

models.User.name: UnassignedPermissions
models.User.doc: UnassignedPermissions returns a slice of unassigned permissions

models.Visit.name: BeforeCreate
models.Visit.doc:
BeforeCreate gorm hook makes sure that we only Queue one visit at a time
for any given patient.

models.Visit.name: AfterCreate
models.Visit.doc:
AfterCreate gorm hook ensures that it created the HMIS 105 entry for each visit
and sends the realtime notification for new visit.
if this fails, the transaction will fail and visit will not be commited by GORM.

models.Visit.name: AfterUpdate
models.Visit.doc: AfterUpdate gorm hook that sends the notification when the patient visit is Queued.

models.WaiverList.name: User
models.WaiverList.doc: User is the adminstrator who authorized the waiver.

routes.Item.name: Name
routes.Item.doc: The inventory item name. It must be unique.

routes.Item.name: Type
routes.Item.doc: Type is the BillableType of the item.

routes.Item.name: CostPrice
routes.Item.doc:
CostPrice is the unit cost of each item or otherwise best called "rate" in commerce.
TODO: The current implementation has a limitation because drugs of different batches have the same cost price.
TODO: The aim if to add a cost price field per batch to fix this issue.

routes.Item.name: Dept
routes.Item.doc:
Dept is the inventory department. It is used to differentiate between lab investigations, radiology tests
pharmacy items (drugs and consumables), nursing care etc.
So when we make a bill, we know where to send it.

routes.Item.name: Quantity
routes.Item.doc:
The Quantity of items in stock. Like CostPrice, this ONLY makes sense for Consumables.
Drug quantity is stored under each batch. BillableTypes other than Consumable ignore this field all together.

routes.Item.name: CreatedAt
routes.Item.doc: The time when the item was inserted into the database.

routes.Item.name: ExpiryDate
routes.Item.doc:
Item expiry date. Expiry dates for drugs are stored per batch, so this is only useful for consumables ONLY.
It will otherwise be the zero value 0001-01-01.

routes.Item.name: MinAlertQuantity
routes.Item.doc: Quantity that should be in stock before an alert is raised.

routes.Item.name: Batches
routes.Item.doc:
DrugBatches for the item (if it is a drug)
For backwards compatibility, we maintain the Quantity Field and the ExpiryDate field here.
Like wise other items that are not drugs don't have DrugBatches.

routes.Item.name: HasValidExpiryDate
routes.Item.doc: HasValidExpiryDate returns true if the item has a valid expiry date.

routes.Item.name: IsExpired
routes.Item.doc:
IsExpired returns true if the item expired.
If the item is not a drug or consumable, it always returns false.

routes.Item.name: Price
routes.Item.doc:
Price returns the inventory item selling price for the given payment type.
If the payment type does not exist, it will return 0 and an error.

routes.Item.name: PriceByID
routes.Item.doc:
PriceByID returns the selling price of the inventory item for the given payment ID.
If the payment type id does not exist, it will return 0 and an error.

routes.Item.name: IsDrug
routes.Item.doc: IsDrug returns true if the item.Type == enums.BillableTypeDrug.

routes.Item.name: IsConsumable
routes.Item.doc: IsConsumable returns true if the item.Type == enums.BillableTypeConsumable.

routes.Item.name: IsDrugOrConsumable
routes.Item.doc: IsDrugOrConsumable returns true if item.IsDrug() || item.IsConsumable().

routes.Item.name: BatchID
routes.Item.doc:
Foreign key to the drug_batch table to indicate the batch of the drug
This is only required for drugs

routes.Prescription.name: AddToChart
routes.Prescription.doc: Flag to indicate whether to add it to treatment chart

views.LabRequest.name: ID
views.LabRequest.doc: ID: Corresponds to the billable id in Billables

views.LabRequest.name: ItemID
views.LabRequest.doc: ItemID: Corresponds to the inventory item id in InventoryItems

views.LabRequest.name: CreatedAt
views.LabRequest.doc: CreatedAt: Date the request was made

views.LabRequest.name: Name
views.LabRequest.doc: Name is the name of the inventory item(eg. FBC, Urinalysis)

views.LabRequest.name: ReportType
views.LabRequest.doc: Possibly null if no report yet

views.LabRequest.name: RequestedBy
views.LabRequest.doc: Requested by is the name of the doctor who requested the test

views.PerPatientBill.name: PatientID
views.PerPatientBill.doc: ID of the client with the debt

visitmanager.InsuranceStats.name: ThisWeek
visitmanager.InsuranceStats.doc: Number of insurance visits this week

visitmanager.InsuranceStats.name: ThisMonth
visitmanager.InsuranceStats.doc: Number of insurance visits this month

visitmanager.InsuranceStats.name: ThisYear
visitmanager.InsuranceStats.doc: Number of insurance visits this year

visitmanager.InsuranceStats.name: Load
visitmanager.InsuranceStats.doc: Load loads the insurance statistics from the database, populating the struct

visitmanager.OTCStats.name: TotalVisits
visitmanager.OTCStats.doc: Total number of paying OTC visits

visitmanager.OTCStats.name: TopIncomeItems
visitmanager.OTCStats.doc: Most items sold by amount made.
